CSCE 399 Syllabus

Section 500 (39488)

High-Impact Experience

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

Meeting Times:  Meeting Type: INS

Meeting Days:

Start Time: N/A

End Time: N/A

Start Date: 01/12/2026

End Date: 05/05/2026

Credit Hours:  0

Instructor Details

Shawna Thomas

Email:  sthomas@tamu.edu

Office Location:  319 Peterson

Phone:  979-862-8877

Office Hours

I would be happy to meet with you. Email me to set up a

time that works for both of us.

Preferred Contact Method

Email. I always want to hear from you, but at certain

times of the semester the volume of email becomes

large. If you email me and don't hear back within two

business days, please send a follow up email. I will

appreciate the kind reminder.

Webpage:  https://sthomas.engr.tamu.edu/

Catalog Description

High-Impact Experience. (0-0). Credit 0. Participation in an approved high-impact

learning practice; documentation and self-assessment of learning experience.

Prerequisite: Junior or senior classification; also taught at Galveston campus.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Junior or senior classification; also taught at

Galveston campus.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, you will be able to:

Identify  key features of your high-impact learning experience

Reflect  on how your high-impact learning experience impacted you

Special Course Designation

None

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Optional

No Textbook Needed

Grading Policy

This course is graded on a pass/fail (S/U) basis.

To pass (S), you must:

have an approved high-impact learning experience completed  and

submit an acceptable self-reflection with acceptable external documentation

by the last day of classes.

The high-impact learning experience must meet the Department of Computer

Science and Engineering's high-impact experience policy (included in the course

materials). The instructor will decide if a submission is “successful” or “incomplete.”

Incomplete submissions will be returned to the student for revision and must be

resubmitted by the last day of classes.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

materials). The instructor will decide if a submission is “successful” or “incomplete.”

Incomplete submissions will be returned to the student for revision and must be

resubmitted by the last day of classes.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

There is no penalty for late submissions, however submissions and resubmissions

will not be accepted after the last day of classes.

Course Schedule

Course assignment dates:

1.  Activity Checker Survey  [due end of week 1, closes last day of classes]: verify

your completed activity qualifies

2.  ENGR[x] Submission (required to pass)  [due end of week 11, closes last

day of classes]: describe your activity, provide external documentation of

successful completion, and reflect on your experience

Your external documentation must be from the sponsor of the

experience and confirm successful completion. (Offer letters for the

activity do not qualify.)

Your self-reflection must follow the format provided on Canvas.

Submissions and resubmissions will not be accepted after the last day of classes.

Additional Course Information

The materials used within this course are copyrighted. These materials include, but

are not limited to, the syllabus, quizzes, assignments, activity descriptions, online

content, course videos, audio and visual recordings of classes, etc. Because these

materials are copyrighted, you do not have the right to copy or distribute these

materials, unless permission is expressly granted.

AI Statement

According to the Texas A&M University Definitions of Academic Misconduct,

plagiarism is the appropriation of another person's ideas, processes, results or

words without giving appropriate credit ( aggiehonor.tamu.edu ). You should credit

your use of anyone else's words, graphic images, or ideas using standard citation

styles.  Artificial Intelligence (AI) text generators and natural language

processing tools (colloquially, chatbots - such as ChatGPT), audio, computer

code, video, and image generators should not be used for any work for this

class  without explicit permission of the instructor and appropriate attribution. This

includes, but is not limited to,

1. Creating or revising drafts

2. Editing your work

3. Reviewing a peer's work

This excludes pre-existing software additions such as spelling and grammar

checkers, which are acceptable.
